column1: # Export Layers To Files — CSV Template
# HOW IT WORKS
# Each row maps 1-to-1 with a Photoshop layer
# Row 1  →  topmost layer
# Row 2  →  second layer from top
# ...and so on.
# The script reads this file
# order
column2: and applies each row's settings to the matching layer.

column1: # The layer count in the PSD must match the row count in the CSV.
# COLUMNS
# Width   — output canvas width in pixels
# Height  — output canvas height in pixels
# Padding — empty space (px) added around the layer content
#            before fitting. Use 0 for no padding.
# Filename — output filename without extension.
#            Use subfolder/name to place the file in a subfolder
#            (the folder is created automatically).
# Mode    — optional
#            fit    : scale the layer to fill the canvas (minus padding)
#            canvas : set canvas to W×H without scaling the layer content
# SPECIAL VALUES
# -ignore  — skips this layer entirely (no file exported).
#            The row still counts against the layer order so all
#            rows below it remain correctly aligned.
#            Use 0
column3: 0 for the W/H/P columns (they are ignored).

Width
# Layer content is scaled to fit inside (Width - Padding*2) x (Height - Padding*2).
# Canvas is then set to exactly Width x Height.
# A full-size background — no padding needed
# A logo scaled to fit a 400×200 box with 20px breathing room on all sides
# A title banner — "fit" is the default so the Mode column can be omitted
# This layer is skipped. No file is created.
# The rows below still line up correctly with the layers below this one.
# Canvas is set to exactly Width x Height.
# The layer content is NOT scaled — it stays at its original size.
# Use this for icons or sprites that must stay pixel-perfect.
